checks.finding: Inline decisions carry rationale: OQ-1 cap_source field is justified 'so skill-instruction-enforced caps ... are registered and inventoried without demanding nonexistent driver scripts ... (INV-004)' (design.md Design Decisions section); OQ-2 fixture_profiles vocabulary is justified via INV-019/ADR-0010; the 'A4 parity-extension placement (decision)' section (design.md lines 200-213) gives an explicit three-part Justification (a)(b)(c) for keeping the parity extension inside loop-escalation.tests.sh instead of editing tests/template-validator-parity.tests.sh; frontend-spec.md's Technology Stack table (lines 9-14) carries a Rationale column for every technology row (cross-host determinism INV-021 for Bash/PowerShell twins; 'already a repository dependency' for jq). No inline technology or architectural statement lacks a because/since-equivalent clause.

design.md ## Open Questions (lines 326-334) carries a single entry, OQ-5, with an explicit owner ('maintainers + the A3 implementing task', not TBD), an explicit 'Blocks Implementation: no' field, and a concrete Resolution Path ('code inspection during implementation of the task-review leg; finding recorded in that task's implementation report; the leg asserts only HEAD-observable behavior until then'). All three required fields are present.

investigation.md is present (INV-001..INV-022), so the absent-investigation branch does not apply. design.md ## Assumptions (lines 315-324) contains three statements, each grounded: '2d8c6a5 stays in mainline ancestry (verified at spec time on feature/epic-159-pillar-a)' traces to INV-012/INV-013; the script-path assumption is explicitly marked '(verified, correcting the INV-001 table's terminal-tier driver path ...)'; the write_contract() fixture-shape assumption cites INV-008 (tests/spec-review-loop.tests.sh:39-99). No ungrounded non-trivial assumption found.

Primary/TYPE-D path applies: design.md ## Constraint Compliance (lines 304-313) is present with six rows covering no-protected-file-modification (Non-goals), .sh/.ps1 twin mandate and cross-host support (REQ-005), no-false-green (harness Risks), doc-following (REQ-006), and version-bump-only-via-script (REQ-006). Security boundaries B1 (fixture isolation / no real repo path written) and B3 (protected-basename+write-verb confinement) from requirements.md's Security Boundaries table are not separate rows in that table but ARE addressed elsewhere in the same document (design.md Security Boundaries section lines 279-287; Data Plan lines 108-118: 'Existing Data Affected: none. Real reports/, specs/ ... are never written by the harness'), so no constraint is contradicted or absent from the design as a whole. The task-ID prefix-collision edge case (AC-018) is addressed at Test Strategy item 3 (design.md lines 244-245).

requirements.md's only numeric performance requirement is AC-017/TEST-017 (each new suite self-fails past a 300-second wall-clock budget). design.md Test Strategy item 1b (lines 224-232) now defines the concrete mechanism: 'assert_runtime_budget <start_epoch>', threshold constant 'LOOP_SUITE_BUDGET_SECONDS=300', measured-seconds printed in the summary line, in-suite self-fail at >300s, and a threshold-0 negative self-check proving the assertion is live. infra-spec.md's Runtime Budget section (lines 66-79) reiterates the identical mechanism and enforcement point and adds per-OS wall-clock recording in implementation reports. design.md Risks (lines 340-345) cross-references both. This resolves round 1's Major finding.

design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan (lines 295-302) states the deployment target ('No runtime deployment ... existing 3-OS matrix', deterministic-lane placement per infra-spec.md's #126 note), the required CI pipeline changes (four suites join tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 and .github/workflows/test.yml), and rollback ('reviewed revert of that item's commit; no human-copy re-copy step'). Feature-flag strategy is not applicable (no incrementally-rolled-out runtime feature — the deliverable is a CI-only test harness). Migration execution order is not applicable because Data Plan states 'Migration Strategy: none' (no schema changes).

design.md ## Data Plan (lines 108-118) states 'Existing Data Affected: none' and 'Migration Strategy: none' — no schema, column, or table changes are declared, satisfying the check's stated PASS condition.

design.md ## External Integrations (lines 289-293) states 'None. No network calls, no new services, no third-party actions,' consistent with the rest of the design — every cited script (validate-review-context-set.sh, check-quality-gate-cycle-limit.sh, select-agent-model.sh, check-terminal-tier-resume.sh) is an internal repository script driven read-only, not an external/third-party integration. The runtime dependencies that could actually fail (bash, pwsh, jq, python3, git) are each named with explicit absence/failure behavior in infra-spec.md's Runtime Dependencies table (lines 47-56).

Every component in design.md's Components table (lines 42-51) — loop-inventory.json, loop-inventory.tests.sh/.ps1, loop-driver.sh/.ps1 + smoke suite, loop-consistency.tests.sh/.ps1, loop-escalation.tests.sh/.ps1, run-all.sh/.ps1 + test.yml edits — traces to REQ-001..REQ-005 via the Cross-Layer Dependencies table (lines 93-98). Every requirements.md Goal (REQ-001..REQ-006) has a corresponding design element (REQ-006 is covered by Constraint Compliance rows 5-6). No component, data model, or endpoint appears without a requirements.md anchor, and no Goal or User Story capability is omitted.

The high-risk surfaces actually present are the two Critical risks in requirements.md (false-green harness drift, cap-drift narrowing) — not security/PII/payment/cross-service surfaces, none of which apply to this read-only, network-free test harness. design.md names concrete validation paths for each: Test Strategy item 1 (TEST-001/002/007/010/012/017/018 negative self-checks that re-run the verification against a mutated mktemp copy and assert red), Test Strategy item 2 (the RED-differential procedure against 2d8c6a5^ with an explicit git-worktree command sequence, AC-009/TEST-009), and security-spec.md's Security Tests table (TEST-005/002/010/011/013/012 mapped to each STRIDE boundary).
